initial_program:
% -------------------------------------------------
% Determine the outermost rows/columns (used for boundary)
maxRow = #max{R : input(R, _, _)}.
maxCol = #max{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Cells on the border that are not colour 3 are starting points for the floodfill
boundary_outside(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V != 3, R = 0.
boundary_outside(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V != 3, R = maxRow.
boundary_outside(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V != 3, C = 0.
boundary_outside(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V != 3, C = maxCol.

% 4connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1).
delta(0,-1).
delta(1,0).
delta(-1,0).

% Outside set  cells reachable from the boundary through cells that are not colour3
outside(R,C) :- boundary_outside(R,C).
outside(R,C) :- outside(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC), R = R1+DR, C = C1+DC,
                input(R,C,V), V != 3.

% Choose the colour of each output cell
cell(R,C,Color) :- input(R,C,V), V = 3, Color = 3.          % colour3 cells stay unchanged
cell(R,C,4)   :- input(R,C,V), V != 3, not outside(R,C), Color = 4.  % interior non3  colour4
cell(R,C,Color) :- input(R,C,V), V != 3, outside(R,C), Color = V.      % outside non3  keep original colour

% Exactly one output cell for every input cell
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- cell(R, C, Color).

rounds.program_after:
% -------------------------------------------------
% Determine the outermost rows/columns (used for boundary)
row(R) :- input(R, C, V).
col(C) :- input(R, C, V).
maxRow = #max{R : row(R)}.
maxCol = #max{C : col(C)}.

% Cells on the border that are not colour 3 are starting points for the floodfill
boundary_outside(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V != 3, R = 0.
boundary_outside(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V != 3, R = maxRow.
boundary_outside(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V != 3, C = 0.
boundary_outside(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V != 3, C = maxCol.

% 4connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1).
delta(0,-1).
delta(1,0).
delta(-1,0).

% Outside set  cells reachable from the boundary through cells that are not colour3
outside(R,C) :- boundary_outside(R,C).
outside(R,C) :- outside(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC), R = R1+DR, C = C1+DC,
                input(R,C,V), V != 3.

% Choose the colour of each output cell
cell(R,C,Color) :- input(R,C,V), V = 3, Color = 3.          % colour3 cells stay unchanged
cell(R,C,4)   :- input(R,C,V), V != 3, not outside(R,C), Color = 4.  % interior non3  colour4
cell(R,C,Color) :- input(R,C,V), V != 3, outside(R,C), Color = V.      % outside non3  keep original colour

% Exactly one output cell for every input cell
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- cell(R, C, Color).

rounds.program_after:
% -------------------------------------------------
% Determine the outermost rows/columns (used for boundary)
row(R) :- input(R, C, V).
col(C) :- input(R, C, V).
maxRow = #max{R : row(R)}.
maxCol = #max{C : col(C)}.

% Cells on the border that are not colour 3 are starting points for the floodfill
boundary_outside(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V != 3, R = 0.
boundary_outside(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V != 3, R = maxRow.
boundary_outside(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V != 3, C = 0.
boundary_outside(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V != 3, C = maxCol.

% 4connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1).
delta(0,-1).
delta(1,0).
delta(-1,0).

% Outside set  cells reachable from the boundary through cells that are not colour3
outside(R,C) :- boundary_outside(R,C).
outside(R,C) :- outside(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC), R = R1+DR, C = C1+DC,
                input(R,C,V), V != 3.

% Choose the colour of each output cell
cell(R,C,Color) :- input(R,C,V), V = 3, Color = 3.          % colour3 cells stay unchanged
cell(R,C,4)   :- input(R,C,V), V != 3, not outside(R,C), Color = 4.  % interior non3  colour4
cell(R,C,Color) :- input(R,C,V), V != 3, outside(R,C), Color = V.      % outside non3  keep original colour

% Exactly one output cell for every input cell
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- cell(R, C, Color).

final_program:
% -------------------------------------------------
% Determine the outermost rows/columns (used for boundary)
row(R) :- input(R, C, V).
col(C) :- input(R, C, V).
maxRow = #max{R : row(R)}.
maxCol = #max{C : col(C)}.

% Cells on the border that are not colour 3 are starting points for the floodfill
boundary_outside(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V != 3, R = 0.
boundary_outside(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V != 3, R = maxRow.
boundary_outside(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V != 3, C = 0.
boundary_outside(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V != 3, C = maxCol.

% 4connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1).
delta(0,-1).
delta(1,0).
delta(-1,0).

% Outside set  cells reachable from the boundary through cells that are not colour3
outside(R,C) :- boundary_outside(R,C).
outside(R,C) :- outside(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC), R = R1+DR, C = C1+DC,
                input(R,C,V), V != 3.

% Choose the colour of each output cell
cell(R,C,Color) :- input(R,C,V), V = 3, Color = 3.          % colour3 cells stay unchanged
cell(R,C,4)   :- input(R,C,V), V != 3, not outside(R,C), Color = 4.  % interior non3  colour4
cell(R,C,Color) :- input(R,C,V), V != 3, outside(R,C), Color = V.      % outside non3  keep original colour

% Exactly one output cell for every input cell
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- cell(R, C, Color).
